mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the high unemployment signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates immediate supply-side contraction and inventory depletion, which forces equity markets to price in lower near-term corporate output and yields negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes during tightening periods produce positive 5-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 220.0, inflationary pressures force central banks to accelerate rate hikes, which compresses equity valuation multiples and triggers immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, thereby driving positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC over 60-day horizons, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when tested against historical early-cycle data.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with improved corporate profit margins and revenue expansion, which consistently generates positive monthly S&P 500 forward returns as equity valuations adjust to stronger economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 105.0 during disinflationary periods, it signals resilient manufacturing capacity without inflationary constraints, which improves corporate margin expectations and produces positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 105.0 during disinflation, or the threshold loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.
